text:
TheParis2024Olympicsdon’t officially get underway until Friday night but already the greatest show on earth has been plagued by several scandalous moments.

Fromathletes who have been bannedto athletes whohaven’tbeen banned, political controversy toenvironmental concerns, the build-up to the Games has beeninterestingto say the least.

While organisers will hope to clear up some of these before the opening ceremony, some issues look set to dominate headlines over the next two-and-a-half weeks.

With that in mind here’s a comprehensive list of the scandals that have rocked Paris 2024 so far:

Charlotte Dujardin isBritain’sjoint-most decorated female Olympian, but was forced to withdraw from the Paris Games after a video came to light which showed herrepeatedly whipping a horse on the legs.

The 39-year-old has been banned for six months by the International Federation for Equestrian Sports (FEI) over the incident which took place in a coaching session four years ago.

In a statement,Dujardin claimed she made an ‘error of judgement’and is ‘deeply ashamed’ of her actions. The scandal has once again raised theissue of the treatment of horses in sports.

To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web
							browser thatsupports HTML5
								video

Up Next

The Netherlands have found themselves under the spotlight afterconvicted rapist Steven van de Velde was selected for their volleyball team.

Van de Velde was sentenced to four years in prison in 2016 afterpleading guilty to raping a 12-year-old girl when he was 19.

The now 29-year-old willnot be staying at the Olympic Villageandcomments from his teammates defending himand the Dutch team boss’ ‘surprise’ at the reaction to his selection have courted more controversy.

While the International Olympic Committee (IOC) permits athletes to wear a hijab, host teamFrance have imposed a ban on their own competitorsfrom wearing the Muslim headscarf. Amnesty International have described the ban as‘racist gender discrimination’.

Israeli athletes will be given round-the-clock security protection at the Olympics after a French MP said they‘are not welcome in Paris’because of the ongoing war in Gaza.

In a related incident,Adidas pulled an advert featuring Bella Hadidthat made reference to the 1972 Munich Olympics where 11 Israeli athletes were killed in a terrorist attack.

Follow us to receive the latest news updates from Metro (Picture: Getty Images)

Metro’s on Whatsapp!Join our communityfor breaking news and juicy stories.

Canada sacked two staff members from their women’s football team after theyused a drone to fly over and film the training sessions of rivalsNew Zealand ahead of their match.

With the opening ceremony, triathlon and marathon swimming events taking place on the Seine, the French government havespent €1.4billion to clean up the riverthat has been illegal to swim in for a century due to high pollution levels.

Has it worked? Well, until very recently the Seine was still failing water quality tests for E coli but organisers are confident it is now safe – that is unless a so-called‘sh*t flashmob’doesn’t happen.

In a bid to make Paris 2024 the greenest games in history, the Olympic village was built with eco-friendly water pumps rather than air conditioning units.

But with temperatures set to surpass 30C, several delegations, including Team GB, have forked out on hiring 2,500 of their own AC units in order to stay cool.

Earlier this year it was revealed that 23 Chinese swimmers – 11 of whom will compete in Paris – had tested positive for banned substance TMZ ahead of the last games in Tokyo.

Though they were cleared to compete, the World Ant-Doping Agency’s handling of the investigation and decision to keep it private for three years has been widely criticised.

title: All the scandals that have rocked the Paris 2024 Olympics before it's even started | Metro News
description: The Paris 2024 Olympics has been marred by scandals including Charlotte Dujardin, Steven van de Velde, Hijab ban and Seine safety fears.
